# Jun 16 2005 at 7:13 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593362181883
22 posts
Trio'd
70 SK - Anguish Geared
70 Beast - Time Geared
68 Cleric - Ep / Time geared

Didn't take too long, cleric had ft32 (cap of course) SK did mana taps and so forth. Me, bst, had paragon set up for lom times.. all had clairvoyance and fully raid buffed.. was pretty easy.
Twinkage =)

Sage
3,705 posts
Of course you trio'd it with an Anguish geared tank, a slower/dps and a cleric.

I helped 5 man Staute with a Qvic geared monk tanking.

I assume it's a typo, but FT cap is 23, not 32.

15 base, plus 5 increase to cap from aa, plus 3 innate mana regen from aa = 23.

Order

# Dec 16 2004 at 5:53 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592245905595
343 posts
If Yahaman was doing this on The Rathe (running pick up raids without a guild organization) he would be blackflagged and never get a group. The Rathe has player made rules. Those rules say your guild has to take out a mob on your first attempt, THEN you go on rotation.

If your caught KS'n or going out of rot, there is a price to pay.

At first I thought you were joking about everything being on a rotation. I looked at the posts date and its not that old. I am thankful I am not on the Rathe. The RTA, or whatever it is, sounds like a bunch of greedy bastages parseling out who can kill what when it suits them. First agg gets the kill. If a bunch of folks want to form up and go after something it's there right. Not the right of a bunch of the "ubers" to sit there and tell them they cant do it.

# Dec 18 2004 at 12:11 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759497888
68 posts
The thing I don't like about the RTA (The Rathe's rotation organization) is that my EQ experience is being limited by other players and that I have *very* little input in the whole system. The system is dominated by the uber guilds....break a rule and feel their anger. I get the point of it and everything but it's *so* restrictive and non-representative.

While many may point out that each guild gets representatives to the RTA...
1) If the rep's push too hard for something the RTA community may grant what they request but they will be frowned upon in future events
2) What about those that aren't guilded?

Edited, Sat Dec 18 00:17:24 2004

QuoteReply
# Sep 19 2004 at 11:00 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756453357
9 posts
Lady Shadrune, the poster who was "sourgraping" did this back in April during the time Yah was not guilded and simply running a pickup raid for non-ubers to get PoP flags and armor upgrades...

We created The Death Squad in late April and are doing great... Yahmahn still does pickup raids on weekends when the guild is not raiding and has gotten countless folks some much needed flags...

There were no flagshare rules on yahraids and they were open to one and all. The loser posting this was just upset that his guild's planned raid of Vindi was thwarted by someone killing him before they could get online to do it... Vindi was only a target because he was needed cleared so the raid could reach the final objective behind him.

Yahraids has killed RZtW on his pickup raids and gotten that flag for people that would have never seen Tactics without Yahmahn's dedicated efforts to help those less fortunate then the players in dedicated hard-core raiding guilds.

TDS is doing fine, thank you...

If you run in one of those guilds that always run half an hour to an hour late, because your waiting on uber dps machine<#^%*(&^*(&)^> to arrive, who is running late because he forgot to stop by McDonalds on his way to the computer from the rest room, and a pick up raid hits it and gets it, before you get there, then you should have spent that half an hour waiting checking on other targets.

Don't waste even more of your precious time coming here flaming someone that helps the other non-UBERS<--Stupid word> get what they want/need.
▲ Page top
Pick up raid
QuoteReply
# Mar 30 2004 at 2:07 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362983603
35 posts
I jus obtained this BP tonight in a pick up raid. On the tribunal , Yahman has the best P/U raids, and everyone really seemed to know what they were doin. The pull went smooth, and granted we had a huge force, we were there for AoW also. All things considered, we were well prepared, and he went down easy. Lots of tanks rolled on this, and i was the lucky one with a 96. I contemplated not rolling, due to owning the Chestplate of Vindication. Of course after i won it, i thought about how foolish that notion really was. Now, i know i could spent days collecting N Ro Ldon points for that sweet BP thats available there. But for some well invested time in a pick up raid, i got this. I'm happy.

By the way , if you play on the tribunal, i highly suggest attending one of Yahman's raids :)

9 posts

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Feb 03 2004 at 1:57 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Well 30mr on the click looks kinda good. There are prolly encounters where it will be more usefull to gain that 30mr and loose 244hp from having no Fot7th. Also note the 10cha on the BoE, it isn't much but it's nice to have. Divine Intervention is based on charm.
Best BP? Not really.
QuoteReply
# Sep 06 2002 at 5:49 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592759422721
39 posts
Is this the best BP in Eq? No, not really. Is it an awesome piece of armor? Hell yeah. It <i>is</i> arguably the best 'Uber' BP when you are speaking about availability, but best total? No.

I would personally say that this is the 4th best BP in Eq. As for a list of what I consider the best, look below.

1. Great Shadow Platemail, +80 Ac, +25 Sta, +125 HP, +30 to SVFIR and SVCOLD, drops off of Aten Ha Ra
2. Heavy Yttrium Breastplate, +75 Ac, +20 Sta, +100 HP, +30 to SVFIR and SVPOS, drops off of Lord Inquisitor Seru
3. Breastplate of the Void, +65 Ac, +25 Sta, +125 HP, effect of Heart of the Void*, +8 to all SV's, drops off of Vyz'Hydra the Cursed
4. Breastplate of Eradication, +55 Ac, +20 Sta, +100 Hp, +8 to all SV's, drops off of the Statue of Rallos Zek

*As for the proc on BPotV, it adds 225 HPs, but does not stack with Shaman HP buffs, meaning that spells such as Khura Focusing (60), which adds 430 HPs, Talisman of Kragg (55), which adds 365-380 HPs and Harnessing of Spirit (49), which adds 248-250 HPs. So, thusly, seeing as most raids buff their MT with at least one of those 3 spells, it isn't truly worth it to sacrifice those 23-205 HPs, so I did not include that in my considerations.

So, although this BP rawks and is alot more accessible than the other 3, it is NOT the best. I still wouldn't turn it down on a raid though =P.
▲ Page top
RE: Best BP? Not really.
QuoteReply

I agree with everything Khift has said here. I do however think that the Warmaster's Ribcage, dropped by a Possessed Warmaster (5th wave ring of fire), also comes close to this BP (+50AC +15Str +15Sta +15Agi +15Dex +15Wis +15Int +15Cha +5vsMg +5vsFire +5vsCold +5vsPos +5vsDis +100HP +100Mana +10.00 Wt), but mainly for Paladins and Shadowknights. This is due to the +15 Int/Wis, +100 Mana and the lighter weight.

Theodus
49 Dwarf Paladin
Fennin Ro by way of Tallon Zek

QuoteReply
# Mar 28 2002 at 1:16 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758390167
4 posts
You should check your facts first Sochi.

True Mithril Breastplate: Wt 8.00. +57 AC, +10 Sta, +20 Agi, +10 Dex, +2 vsMg, +2 vsFire, +2 vsCold, +2 vsPos, +2 vsDis, +50 HP, LORE MAGIC NODROP

It has half the stamina and hp of the Statue BP, with only 2 more AC.

BP of Eradication is still the best melee BP in the game with the Stamina and HP combo. Nice stats.
▲ Page top
RE: One of the best..but not THE best
QuoteReply
# Apr 27 2002 at 1:10 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762086122
235 posts
Dunno that i'd call either of these clearly the best... I also kinda like the chestplate of fiery might. 60ac, lots of stats, effect fiery might. Personally, BP of Eradication would be my choice for most scenarios due to the wicked combination of ac, hp/sta, and stats/resists. If you can get any of the three, consider yourself lucky, they are all insanely badass and hard to get.

Something Funny!
QuoteReply
# Jan 29 2002 at 8:54 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591774310252
2 posts
Strangely enough (and i know someone will flame this comment) I have heard of an uber guild twinking one of their lvl 5 warriors with this breastplate. Statue is commonly pulled to zone when we kill him on our server so it is very possible for a "twink" to be camped there and loot it when the fight is done.. I guess the guild tanks all had equiv or better to do this so I think it would be hilarious seeing a baby warrior roaming around with something like this if I were them hehe :)

Personally I would prefer this BP over any of the others for my warrior as it has the best stamina and nice resists and being a poor little wood elf with around 130stam it helps nicly for the HP's..

59th Warrior
Cazic Thule

RE: Something Funny!
QuoteReply
# Feb 21 2002 at 2:19 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965854709
133 posts
I couldn’t see that happening. This is, in most peoples eyes, the single best breastplate in the game for a warrior.

Also, it is a lot easier fighting him just down the walkway from where he spawns...pulling to zone sounds like a hassle to me. Put his face in the ice wall there around the arena, get him slowed, then its smooth sailing from there on out.

Not too hard really. I like to think that after killing Vindi, the Statue to me is considered, your prize...

# Mar 08 2002 at 4:29 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1638145982070
View User Forum
Sage
201 posts
While I agree this isn't an item that would or should get twinked out. My guild had three drop last night. Now if that happens to us a few more times.....we might be forced to twink it out, or auction off the loot rights. Alright I know it'll never happen but I can dream right?
▲ Page top
RE: Something Funny!
QuoteReply
# Sep 23 2002 at 1:34 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591768298219
View User Forum
4 posts
Who's to say what "should" and "shouldn't" be twinked out. Why should others perscribe the way we choose to play. This is a game.. I should be here for what I define as fun. Personally I hate "no drop" items, or items with level restrictions.

If a lvl 1 player has earned enough cash to buy this item of someone that wants to sell it, who does it hurt for that exchange to take place? Chances are that lvl 1 player has seen enough of the game to appreciate it. Chances are the seller has something better that he wants to replace the bp with.

What a waste to see an item like this rot because it was no-drop. It happens.
▲ Page top
RE: Something Funny!
QuoteReply
# Apr 26 2003 at 12:59 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591788653851
52 posts
errr if that persons main is high enough to obtain an item that would benifet their alt, so lovingly referred to as 'twinks', imo they have earned it once, and should not have to do so again. I have always had somewhat average gear, some slots far less than average, but when i have a decent item i dont need anymore, you'll likely find it winding up on a level 1 character. The only thing wrong with twink is the fact that it causes some new players to feel like ****, making them believe they are not good enough because they dont have 2-3k per slot to blow, when they should instead focus on levels and realise that they arent expected to :)
▲ Page top
why?
QuoteReply
# Nov 15 2001 at 9:26 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248132329
41 posts
Why do you all argue over who deserves to get this high-end no-drop stuff? These are big raid type stuff and usually it is decided who gets what before the first blow is landed...and its always by some guy thats like lvl 32...like they would know heh
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 06 2002 at 12:00 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) in actuality the guild will decide over a RPP sytem (Raid Point) and u " BUY " ur gear
RE: why?
QuoteReply
# Jan 23 2002 at 6:12 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251769110
18 posts
I've heard of RPP systems before. Could you explain in more detail how that would work? thanks in advance. =)
▲ Page top
RE: why?
QuoteReply
# Feb 03 2002 at 11:27 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591765414838
55 posts
Raid points suck imo, but here ya go:

-go on raids to earn points
-item drops
roll on item if u want
-if u win, deduct the proper number of points from your point bank
-sometimes donate old equipment to the guild

thats the short and ugly... sycks if u cant raid much... rules if you can...

▲ Page top
RE: why?
QuoteReply
# May 16 2003 at 3:13 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1595780131429
464 posts
All point systems do not work that way. We don't roll at all unless theres a tie. Point systems are designed for the specific purpose of putting the equipment where it will be used the most (i.e. those who raid the most will earn the most points) while being totally impartial at the same time. They may suck if you only play once a month, but I'd be pretty pissed off if someone who never raided beat me on an item like this when I put in the time to get it and they never show up.

It's all in your perspective.
▲ Page top
RE: why?
QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2002 at 12:27 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592246024631
50 posts
My Guilds point system works like this:

All members were given points to start based on time in guild to a maximum of 11, 1 point per month in guild.

Then all loot we could possibly attain is given a point value.

Vindi BP and this BP are 50 points.
HoT armor is 5-20points
etc etc.

When an item drops, all people who want it send a tell to the loot master, loot master checks point lists, and person with the Highest point total recieves the loot, and has the value deducted from his points (you can go negative if you are the highest who bid on item)

At the end of the raid, all points spent are added up, and divided among all attendees.

So, on a HoT raid with lots of armor, say 20 people were there, and 60 points were spent on loot. Each person in attendance would recieve 3 points.

In ties you would roll etc.

seems to work so far, the people who attend the most raids, get the most points and therefore, most loot.

Rewards participation, but has drawbacks.
▲ Page top
RE: why?
QuoteReply
# May 15 2002 at 12:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591774257848
20 posts
Umm, i think he means go on raids get points and if something you want drops then it has a set point amont then people can bid higher if they want it till theres only one person left.
▲ Page top
Best BP ? hmm Possibly not
QuoteReply
# Nov 15 2001 at 7:11 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758414225
View User Forum
46 posts
This is probably the best Chest Piece in the game for a tank. Second only maybe to the Chestplate of Vindication, and the Chestplate of Fiery Might, both of which have slightly inferior stats overall, but both of these items have their own pretty cool effects. So if your a stats over effects man, go for this BP, but IMHO the Chestplate of Fiery Might is the best Chestplate (Certainly for Warriors) out there.

Ironic how you name is Sanitylost, becuase you have lost your sanity. Think your some big shot, STFU newbie, funny how you dont leave a signature. Only one on Fennin Ro belongs to a BARD. Why, you ask? Becuase 99% of guilds who kill Statue can go to Temple of Veeshan and comeplete Skyshrine armour dip ****. Which means, most the warriors and tanks HAVE ToV armour and no need for this. ▲ Page top
RE: newbies
QuoteReply
# May 02 2002 at 9:51 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762086122
235 posts
Umm... This totally OWNS the ToV BP's for tanks.

I'm not saying bards don't deserve it, there's ALOT of mobs that practically require occlusion to be killed; and bards have a number of other uses. To say that this would go to a bard by default cause all the tanks have better is completly rediculous IMHO. I'd be loathe to say any tank has better, but certainly not all. The only item that can really argue with it is the chestplate of fiery might, but 5ac isnt worth all the stats/hp you get off this baby. True mithril is DEFINATELY not better, there's no way 2ac would beat out the enormous stats and good resists on this. Also, from everything I've ever seen, bards/clerics get ToV BPs first while wariors get this and pallies/SKs get this or vindi BP... the most logical distribution I might add.

Edited, Thu Jun 27 01:09:21 2002

QuoteReply
# Jan 24 2003 at 2:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361611458
8 posts
About the whole bards using this BP thing. Bards get agro when charm/mezz breaks, bards get agro when we OoS, bards get agro when we just stand there singing CoR. But unlike chanters, when our mezz breaks, and we get agro, we dont die in 3 seconds, we last long enough for the tank to regain agro.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 05 2001 at 2:47 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Sanitylost, your a ******* idiot, Bards dont die that much, a smart bard almost never dies. bards solo on my server, Solusek ro, in Sebilis ALOT, wnat me to name a few? Arazanos, Burninsun, Tilion, Prysenius, Linden, Relic, Dont even start about saying bards die whne they get agroed. THEY CAN CHARM AND MEZ TARD! do you think your gonna die if you have a pet fighting it, then mez his buddy and run? if you do your retarded and shouldnt play everquest

#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 30 2001 at 1:57 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) not against things like the statue lol ;P quad 1200's ... dead bard one round. heh
RE: BARDS?
QuoteReply
# Jun 30 2001 at 5:41 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592756557165
11 posts
Stupid argument there, paladin and shadowknights will last all of maybe 1 more hit than a bard against the statue. If the statue agroes on a paladin or sk they are likely dead. Against exp mobs I wear near full melodic and tank better than some warriors, I have compared my bar movement and a warriors against the same type of monster and I dropped slower and I know I have less hp.

This is a who has merit or DKC points item.

These arguments are silly and usually wrong on all accounts.
▲ Page top
RE: BARDS?
QuoteReply
# Apr 20 2002 at 10:32 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592757765879
Scholar
23 posts
And a warrior might live one more hit than a Pally or SK without a CHeal rotation + spams if defensive is burned.
▲ Page top
RE: BARDS?
QuoteReply
# Jun 30 2001 at 10:13 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591751566315
Scholar
18 posts
Heaven forbid that a bard might ever be in a different battle than the one he could get this breastplate. In another situation the extra AC may help him stay alive longer. Or heaven forbid he tries to pull an add off the enchanter or cleric while the primary tanks are busy elsewhere. Every class listed on the item should have an opportunity to roll for it.

It is silly to play a game where people assume every AC item goes to a warrior, every 0.0 weight item goes to a monk, and every INT item goes to a caster. If this were true, most loot would never be available to the other classes and all the paladins, shadowknights, rogues, bards, etc., would still be wearing banded armor or leather. Be nice, play fair, and let all the people who qualify for the item have a chance at it.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 02 2001 at 7:23 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Also dont forget in some areas, when you dont have a chanter, a bard will be playing the part of your crowd controller.

now if you were actually in a position to kill the statue of rallos zek...you would understand that the first person to engage the statue besides your MT would be a bard to occlusion him. statue can be slowed but occlusion and tash are kinda necessary for this. pretty much that way on all high end mobs. the ac the 100hps 20 sta(more ac/hps) and 10 cha make this very useful for a bard.
